hero.title: How children develop digital literacy with Amy Surfwing
hero.lead: This page explains the educational model behind Amy Surfwing: how children develop digital literacy step by step through story, reflection, and real-life digital situations.

goal.title: Children develop digital literacy with Amy Surfwing
goal.body: With Amy Surfwing, we pursue a clear goal: children make independent and responsible decisions online.

learningModel.kicker: How digital literacy develops step by step
learningModel.title: The Amy Surfwing learning model
learningModel.intro: Amy Surfwing is the first learning app to combine storytelling and gamification with child-friendly self-reflection. This helps children develop key skills and apply them more consciously in their digital lives over time.
learningModel.logic: Digital life → Story and reflection → Digital skills → Core dimensions → Digital literacy
learningModel.detailsTitle: The five steps at a glance

0.title: Digital life
0.summary: Children encounter real situations from their digital everyday lives.
0.details: Amy Surfwing addresses topics such as class chats, cyberbullying, self-presentation, AI, gaming, and digital balance. Children learn through situations that are directly relevant to their lives, not abstract theory.

1.title: Story and reflection
1.summary: Situations are experienced through characters and reflected on with Amy.
1.details: Children experience digital situations within an ongoing story world. Reflection questions encourage them to consider different perspectives, express their own thoughts, and question the characters’ behavior.

2.title: Digital skills
2.summary: Key skills are practiced and applied during the learning process.
2.details: Children learn to evaluate information, recognize fake news, communicate respectfully, protect themselves online, and understand media critically.

3.title: Learning spiral
3.summary: Children gradually develop deeper understanding and confidence in their actions.

4.title: Inner development
4.summary: Children develop perspective-taking, judgment, responsibility, and self-efficacy.
4.details: Through repeated learning, children strengthen perspective-taking, judgment, responsibility, self-efficacy, and appropriate decision-making.

5.title: Digital literacy
5.summary: Children act increasingly independently and responsibly.
5.details: Digital literacy is shown when children better understand digital situations, reflect on them, and act more consciously and responsibly in everyday life.

learningModel.resultBody: Amy Surfwing translates educational principles into a format that is understandable, motivating, and relevant for children. Digital education becomes something children actively experience, not just something that is explained.

model.title: How does digital literacy develop?
model.intro: Digital literacy develops when children build key digital skills and apply them reflectively in realistic digital situations.
model.logic: Real-life digital situations are the starting point. Children practice essential skills, reflect on them, and gradually develop core inner abilities that lead to digital literacy.

fields.title: Real-life digital situations
fields.intro: Children need digital literacy not in theory, but in real-life situations.

skills.title: Key digital competencies
skills.intro: These competencies are based on DigComp (EU), MIL (UNESCO), and the Media Competence Framework NRW, adapted in language for our model. They are practiced and applied throughout the learning process.

items.0: Evaluate sources and recognize fake news
items.5: Understand and reflect on media critically

dimensions.title: Core inner abilities of digital literacy
dimensions.intro: When children apply key skills in realistic situations and reflect on their own behavior, they gradually develop the inner abilities that define digital literacy.

0.title: Perspective-taking
0.body: Children learn to understand different viewpoints and see situations from multiple perspectives.

1.title: Judgment
1.body: Children learn to evaluate situations, weigh information, and form their own opinions.

2.title: Responsibility
2.body: Children learn to consider the consequences of their actions and act responsibly online.

3.title: Self-efficacy
3.body: Children learn that their actions matter and that they can influence situations through their own decisions.

principles.title: Educational foundation
principles.intro: Amy Surfwing is based on a combination of established learning principles that shape how children understand, reflect on, and learn from digital situations.

target.title: Focus on children aged 10 to 12
target.body: The current focus is on children aged 10 to 12. At this stage, many begin to navigate the digital world independently: first experiences, more responsibility, uncertainties, and new dynamics in class chats, gaming, social media, and trends.
target.body2: Amy Surfwing supports children exactly in this transition phase: preparing, guiding, and staying close to everyday life.
target.note: Additional age groups are planned for the future.
